# COMP 110 EX09: Continuous Improvement Analysis

## My Idea
The course should include examples in classes or exercises integrating social media or social media example because it will help make the course more relevant to social media users and more engaging.

## Summary of Analysis
To test my idea, I used three columns from the COMP 110 survey. These were hours_online_social, or how many hours a day a student is on social media, interesting, which is how interesting the students thinks the course is 1-7, and interested-connections, which is how interested by student is in connections between CS and other fields 1-7. My hypotheses was that the more a person used social media, the less interested they were in COMP110 and therefore how it could also fit in with other fields. This would lead to the conclusion that by increasing social media examples in class or some other form of integration, students would be more engaged. I then fixed most of the data by removing rows with missing values, then provided visusalizations to prove my point.

## Conclusion

Based on the research, I found that social media usage and interest in the intersection between COMP110 and other fields is not linked, therefore, according to this research, including social media wouldn't improve interest in COMP110 and other fields. The box plot for interesting showed the same results across the board for all social media groups, showing that regardless of social media usage, students showed the same interest in COMP 110. The bar plot of interested_connections was very similar, with the scores being roughly the same and students all having about the same level of interest in the connections between COMP 110 and other fields. However, an important distinction is that the majority of the data comes from people in the social media group 3-5, so anthing below or above that is underrepresented proportional to the data.

Despite the hypthosesis failing, there are still several things to consider when implementing social media into COMP110. Students who use social media less than 3 hours can feel out of place or not connected to the course if social media is implemented in to COMP110. Also, it might be difficult to implement, as the younger generation of students might be more in tune to one side of social media while the older TAs who would help design the course could be on a different side. Also, this could feel like a cheap gimmick to many students and disvalue their idea of COMP110 in their minds.

This hypothesis could be strengthened with better data regarding interest in actually integrating social media in to COMP110, such as a specific question in next year's survery with this as one of the questions. Furthermore, it would be a good idea to have a pilot run, where a social media example is used in class or in one of the exercises and students are asked to fill out an interest form whether it was engaing or not. Finally, gathering more input from other data groups than 3-5 social media hours a day would be useful to actually drawing conclusions.
